channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IMF Agreement with Pakistan Amid Rising Global Energy Turmoil
item.description: The latest staff-level agreement between the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and Pakistan has come at a most uncertain and volatile time. What under normal circumstances would have been taken as a r...

item.title: Pakistan Ports See Surge in Petroleum Handling Amidst Middle East Tensions
item.description: Yaar, things are getting a bit interesting at Pakistan's ports, especially Port Qasim. Because of Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz after those US-Israel attacks, shipping lines are diverting, and Por...

item.title: Three Palestinians killed in Israeli air strikes amid ongoing Gaza violence
item.description: Local health authorities in Gaza have reported that three Palestinian men were killed in separate Israeli air strikes, highlighting continuing violence despite a ceasefire that has been in place for o...

item.title: Kwankwaso and Kwankwasiyya Movement plan move from NNPP to ADC ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: The Kwankwasiyya Movement don tell all their members across Nigeria make dem go join the African Democratic Congress (ADC). According to wetin Dr. Habibu Sale Mohammed, wey be spokesperson for the mov...

item.title: FIA Stops Two Passengers in Multan Over Misuse of Umrah Visas for Illegal Travel to Europe
item.description: The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) in Pakistan took action at Multan International Airport after discovering two passengers allegedly attempting illegal travel to Europe under the guise of perform...

item.title: Debate over Jammu University’s removal of Jinnah, Iqbal, and Syed Ahmad Khan from syllabus highlights threat to academic freedom
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding Jammu University’s recommendation to remove references to Muhammad Ali Jinnah, Sir Syed Ahmad Khan, and Muhammad Iqbal from its MA Political Science s...

item.title: Pakistan, Turkiye and Egypt lead mediation efforts amidst US-Israel conflict with Iran
item.description: The article by Abbas Nasir discusses the escalating conflict between Iran and the US-Israel alliance and the faint possibility of peace emerging through mediation led by Pakistan, Turkiye, and Egypt. ...

item.title: The rise of AI surveillance in West Africa and its impact on migrants’ rights
item.description: West Africa has long been one of the world’s most mobile regions, with citizens of ECOWAS member states enjoying visa‑free travel since 1979. This freedom has promoted trade, labour mobility, and soci...

item.title: JD Vance Tops CPAC Poll as Republican Support Consolidates Behind Him and Rubio
item.description: Vice-President JD Vance has emerged as the leading choice among conservative activists to become the next Republican presidential candidate, according to the 2026 Conservative Political Action Confere...

item.title: Anti-Trump protests in US grow amid criticism of Iran war
item.description: Massive demonstrations erupted across the United States on Saturday as part of the third “No Kings” rallies, organised by groups critical of President Donald Trump’s policies. These events, described ...

item.title: Pakistan’s exports to Europe stagnate despite GSP+ and new trade headwinds
item.description: Pakistan’s exports to key Western European markets are losing momentum despite the country’s preferential GSP+ access, raising concerns about the long‑term demand for Pakistani products in Europe. The...

item.title: Rise in Gilgit-Baltistan tourism amid regional tensions
item.description: Despite fears that the ongoing war in the Middle East and the surge in fuel prices might hurt Gilgit-Baltistan’s tourism sector, the region is witnessing an impressive comeback. According to officials...

item.title: Nestle Reports Significant KitKat Theft in Europe
item.description: Nestlé has reported a significant theft of KitKat products, with approximately 12 tonnes of the chocolate bars stolen from a distribution center in Europe. The exact location and timing of the theft h...

item.title: Pakistan hosts key diplomatic talks to ease US-Iran tensions and support regional peace
item.description: Islamabad has emerged as a central platform for renewed diplomatic efforts aimed at reducing tensions between the United States and Iran after nearly a month of hostilities. The Pakistani government h...

item.title: Pakistan dismisses India's comments on treatment of Shia community, urges focus on internal issues
item.description: Pakistan’s Foreign Office has firmly rejected India’s recent remarks regarding the treatment of the Shia community in Pakistan, terming them 'cynical, diversionary' and a politically motivated attempt...

item.title: Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia injures at least 10 U.S. troops as additional American forces deploy to the region
item.description: An Iranian missile and drone attack targeted Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 28, 2026, wounding at least 10 to 12 U.S. service members, with two reported in serious condition. The stri...

item.title: How Flooring Upgrades Can Affect Your Home’s Value
item.description: Updating your home's flooring can be a strategic way to increase its market value without undertaking major renovations. Hardwood flooring remains the top choice for boosting resale value, offering a ...

item.title: Iran Agrees to Allow Additional 20 Pakistani-Flagged Ships Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar announced late on Saturday that Iran has agreed to permit 20 more ships flying the Pakistani flag to pass through the strategically important Strai...

item.title: Tanzania assures steady fuel supply amid global market pressures
item.description: The Tanzanian government has provided assurance to citizens that the country has sufficient fuel reserves to sustain local demand for at least two to three months, even as international markets face i...

item.title: British investigators charge eighth suspect in Shahzad Akbar home attack case
item.description: British authorities have charged Dylan Martin, 33, in connection with last year’s violent incident targeting the Cambridge home of Shahzad Akbar, who served as former Pakistani prime minister Imran Kh...
